document.title: Software Validation Report for HnVUE
standards: FDA Quality System Regulation 21 CFR Part 820
FDA Quality System Regulation 21 CFR Part 803
CDRH Guidance: General Principles for Software Validation
CDRH Guidance: Use of Off-the-Shelf Software in Medical Devices
CDRH Guidance: Guidelines for Submitting Premarket Notifications for Medical Image Management Devices

sourceSummary.oneLine: Software Validation Report A-SVR-HNV Rev.0 documents validation, requirements, design, testing, traceability, and hazard analysis for HnVUE console software used with the HnX-R1 digital flat panel X-ray detector.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Technical documentation supporting software validation, verification, traceability, hazard analysis, and regulatory submission evidence for HnVUE.
keyPoints: HnVUE is described as console software for a Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector, model HnX-R1.
The report concludes a Moderate level of concern for the software under FDA software premarket submission guidance.
The software requirements cover DICOM, image acquisition, image processing, viewer, worklist, study list, settings, error handling, logging, and equipment control functions.
Verification and validation testing includes original code test, module test, dynamic test, and function test, with listed pass results.
Hazard analysis assigns Class B risk classifications and residual risk acceptability after risk control measures.
Unresolved anomalies are reported as None.

concepts.name: Software Validation Report
concepts.evidence: Document overview: This document is a Software Validation Report for HnVUE, the console software for the Digital Flat Panel X-ray detector.

concepts.name: Moderate Level of Concern
concepts.evidence: According to below responds, we conclude a Moderate level of concern applies to the AXD software. Level of Concern: Moderate

concepts.name: DICOM Export and PACS Transfer
concepts.evidence: With the data export feature, you can export images in DICOM, BMP, JPG, TIFF formats. DICOM image files are transferred to the PACS storage server.

evidence.claim: The document is Software Validation Report A-SVR-HNV Rev.0 for HnVUE.
evidence.evidenceText: Document No. A-SVR-HNV Rev. No. 0 Software Validation Report; Software Validation Report for HnVUE; Report No.: A-SVR-HNV (Rev.0)

evidence.claim: The document revision date and approval dates are July 16, 2025.
evidence.evidenceText: Rev. Date July 16, 2025; Prepared R&D Manager KyungSoo LEE July 16, 2025; Review RA Team Jimin HAN July 16, 2025; Approval Quality Manager Namkyu HUR July 16, 2025

evidence.claim: HnVUE is console software for a Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector, model HnX-R1.
evidence.evidenceText: Product name: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector (Console software) Model name: HnX-R1 Ref no.: HnVUE

evidence.claim: The document states HnVUE stores, enlarges, reduces, views, analyzes, transmits, and outputs medical images.
evidence.evidenceText: HnVUE, a Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector (Console Software), is a software used in devices that store, enlarge, reduce, view, analyze, transmit, and output medical images.

evidence.claim: The software Level of Concern is Moderate under FDA software premarket submission guidance.
evidence.evidenceText: The following section includes the information required in the FDA guidance document entitled “Guidance for the Content of Premarket Submissions for Software Contained in Medical Devices”, May 11, 2005. According to below responds, we conclude a Moderate level of concern applies to the AXD software. Level of Concern: Moderate

evidence.claim: The development process is stated to fulfill 21 CFR 820.30 Design Controls.
evidence.evidenceText: The development process fulfils the requirements 21 CFR 820.30 Design Controls.

evidence.claim: HnVUE development uses C#, Visual Studio, .NET Framework, and SQLite.
evidence.evidenceText: Development environment: Windows 10 64-bit Professional Edition .NET Framework 4.8 .NET Framework 8 C# Visual Studio 2022 C++ Visual Studio 2019 (v142) SQLite 3.46.1

evidence.claim: HnVUE deployment environment includes Windows 10 and Windows 11 64-bit Professional editions.
evidence.evidenceText: Deployment environment: Windows 10 64-bit Professional Edition. Windows 11 64-bit Professional Edition.

evidence.claim: Image data is transferred and stored as 16-bit little-endian two-dimensional arrays without a specific header.
evidence.evidenceText: All image data must be transferred and stored as 16-bit little-endian (Intel byte order) two-dimensional arrays without a specific header.

evidence.claim: The requirements include DICOM standard, image acquisition, image processing, viewer, worklist, study list, configuration, error handling, logging, common, and equipment control groups.
evidence.evidenceText: SRS 100...199 – DICOM Standard; SRS 200...299 – Image Acquisition; SRS 300...299 – Image Processing; SRS 400...499 – Viewer; SRS 500...599 – Worklist; SRS 600...699 – Study List; SRS 700...799 – Configuration; SRS 800...899 – Error Handling; SRS 900...999 – Logging and Common; ASRS 1000...1099 – Equipment Control

evidence.claim: HnVUE supports DICOM export and PACS transfer.
evidence.evidenceText: With the data export feature, you can export images in DICOM, BMP, JPG, TIFF formats. DICOM image files are transferred to the PACS storage server.

evidence.claim: HnVUE supports image acquisition from the detector by Ethernet communication.
evidence.evidenceText: Acquire images captured from the detector via Ethernet communication.

evidence.claim: HnVUE supports multi-detector operation up to three detectors.
evidence.evidenceText: Multi-detector support - up to 3 detectors

evidence.claim: HnVUE supports DCM, DB, and log encryption features.
evidence.evidenceText: Enable encryption for information. Encrypt DCM, DB, Log.

evidence.claim: The software lifecycle is described as a classic waterfall methodology.
evidence.evidenceText: Software development process at software follows a classic waterfall methodology

evidence.claim: Testing is categorized into static analysis and dynamic analysis.
evidence.evidenceText: We categorize our tests into two groups: Static analysis – expert review and analysis of software source code and DEBUG releases. Dynamic analysis – software execution for test data collection and processing.

evidence.claim: The reference list includes EN 62304, EN ISO 14971, EN ISO 13485, FDA 21 CFR Parts 820 and 803, CDRH guidance, and IEEE software standards.
evidence.evidenceText: Reference ● EN 62304:2006/AC: 2008... ● EN ISO 14971:2012... ● EN ISO 13485:2016/AC:2016... ● FDA Quality System Regulation 21 CFR, Part 820 ● FDA Quality System Regulation 21 CFR, Part 803 ● CDRH Guidance... ● IEEE 1012-1986... ● IEEE 829-1983... ● IEEE 1016... ● IEEE 830

evidence.claim: The original code test summary says all functions supporting the system specification are adequate and software verification was performed appropriately.
evidence.evidenceText: Summary: As a result of the code review, we prove that all functions supporting the system specification are adequate... we confirm that the software verification was performed appropriately.

evidence.claim: The dynamic test table records pass results for listed dynamic test items.
evidence.evidenceText: 8.2.3. Dynamic test # Test item Test criteria Pass(P) Fail(F)... A Run the software... P ... I Check image quality... P

evidence.claim: The function test table records pass results for listed installation, patient registration, image acquisition, image processing, DICOM transfer, export, and display tests.
evidence.evidenceText: 8.2.4. Function test # Test item Test criteria Pass(P) Fail(F) A Installation... P ... S Check, Application Features - Image Adjustment (Reset, Rotate, Flip, Invert) P

evidence.claim: Hazard analysis identifies software risk classes as Class B for listed hazards.
evidence.evidenceText: 10.4. Hazard Analysis Requirement Software item Description Risk class Risk ID... Memory Management... Class B HAZ 0001; SRS 100 DICOM Standard... Class B HAZ 0002

evidence.claim: Risk acceptability defines acceptable risk levels 1 through 4 and unacceptable risk levels 5 through 25.
evidence.evidenceText: Acceptable – Light Gray Zone (Risk Level 1 ~ 4) In this zone, risk is acceptable. Unacceptable – Dark Gray Zone (Risk Level 5 ~ 25) Elimination or mitigation actions must be taken

evidence.claim: HnVUE software version information lists version 1.0.0 as the initial release dated July 16, 2025.
evidence.evidenceText: Software version info Revision Date Name Description 1.0.0 July 16, 2025 HnVUE Initial release

evidence.claim: The unresolved anomalies section lists no unresolved anomalies.
evidence.evidenceText: 13.2. Unresolved Anomalies No Problem Description Criticality Reason for Problem Potential Impact None

reviewFlags.reason: Authority context is ambiguous: source metadata indicates MFDS/domestic and the path references FDA 510(k), while the document text cites FDA guidance, CDRH guidance, and 21 CFR but is not itself issued by a regulator.
Product/software naming conflict: the report is for HnVUE, but the Level of Concern and Hazard Analysis sections refer to AXD.
OCR or translation quality is uneven, with malformed or inconsistent phrases such as duplicated wording, mismatched expected results, and non-technical text in hazard controls.
Document date, revision, report number, product model, and software version are present in the source text.
